reason: Both correctly land on "fix, not kill" with similar structure and share several key points (LTV:CAC ~2.2 marginal, negative keywords, keyword/campaign pruning, conversion-tracking fixes, kill triggers). A is stronger on precision: it works out payback more rigorously (accounting for gross margin), explicitly calls out blended vs. per-campaign CAC hiding high-cost losers, and adds a sharp, correct back-of-envelope churn calculation (LTV/price implies ~7-8%/mo churn, well above B2B SaaS norms) showing that fixing retention could flip the LTV:CAC ratio to 5.5:1 — a genuinely useful reframing of the diagnosis. B is more concise and does explicitly mention comparing against alternative channels (SEO, outbound, partnerships) in its kill trigger, which A omits entirely — the one point where B is more complete. But B's payback math is vaguer and it never addresses blended-vs-paid CAC. On balance, A's extra analytical depth (churn insight, blended CAC) is more decision-relevant than B's channel-comparison mention, giving A a modest edge.
